task_completion.description: Agent views emails to identify those related to software releases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening, reading, or previewing emails in the inbox that contain release-related keywords (e.g., release, version, v2, announce, candidate, build).

task_completion.description: At least three relevant emails are starred or flagged.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The inbox list view shows star or flag icons actively highlighted/selected next to at least three different emails that match the release criteria.

task_completion.description: A draft email is created and addressed to the correct recipient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new email composition or draft window is open with 'product-team@company.com' entered in the 'To' or recipient field.

task_completion.description: The draft email has the correct subject line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draft email window shows 'Community Release Feedback' (or at least contains the words 'release' and 'feedback') in the Subject field.

task_completion.description: The draft email body lists the exact subject lines of the starred emails.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main text area of the draft email contains a list of subject lines. These lines must exactly match the subjects of the three emails that were starred/flagged in the inbox.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical email client to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard GUI email application (like Thunderbird, Evolution, or webmail) being used, rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interactions.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'grep', 'echo', or 'curl' being used to search emails or create the draft.

integrity.description: The agent copied actual email subjects from the inbox rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The subject lines pasted into the draft body correspond to actual emails visible in earlier screenshots of the inbox, not generic or fabricated text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Relevant emails must contain at least one of the following keywords in their subject or body: 'release', 'version', 'v2', 'announce', 'candidate', 'build'. At least 3 such emails must be starred/flagged. The draft email must be addressed to 'product-team@company.com'. The draft subject must contain the words 'release' and 'feedback' (e.g., 'Community Release Feedback'). The draft body must contain the exact subject lines of the 3 starred emails.
